product_truth: Stratum still exposes the legacy live ATS-to-LLM dashboard path, but Phases 1 through 5 of the migration are complete. The system now has foundation infra, frozen provider capture, report-run worker orchestration, structured analysis with claims and citations, and report publication with a stored read path. It must still become an immutable point-in-time intelligence report system through later phases.

chosen_stack.auth: Auth.js with Google OIDC and JWT sessions

completed_work: Central env handling added in src/lib/env.ts
Postgres connection setup added in src/db/client.ts
Drizzle schema scaffolding added for tenants, users, memberships
Provider freeze path added for raw ATS payload capture and normalized job persistence
Report-run creation and status modules added
Postgres row claiming with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED added
Worker snapshot-stage execution verified in the local environment
Standalone worker now loads .env.local/.env and exits cleanly in --once mode
Structured analysis added for frozen report-run inputs
Stored published report read path added
critical_rules: Do not continue beyond the next approved phase
No live ATS or AI calls on the future report read path
Keep legacy env usage isolated until later phases explicitly replace it
forbidden_scope: Do not skip ahead beyond Phase 6
Do not implement HTML/PDF artifacts outside the approved artifact phase
Do not refactor unrelated UI for polish
known_risks: The live ATS-to-LLM product path still exists and remains non-enterprise-safe
Auth foundation was built but not browser-verified end-to-end in this conversation
One unrelated lint warning remains in src/components/ui/ServiceInterruptionModal.tsx
AWS vars are optional now but become required when snapshot/artifact storage starts
A hosted Postgres vendor such as Neon was not actually established in the approved source truth
